hooks.command: echo 'AIMFP plugin active. The AIMFP MCP server provides 220 tools for FP-compliant development and project management. Call aimfp_run() as your first action on every interaction. See the aimfp-mode skill for full behavioral rules.'

hooks.prompt: A file was just written or edited via the Write or Edit tool. AIMFP requires all source code files to be tracked in project.db via the reserve-write-finalize workflow. Check the tool input from $ARGUMENTS: is this a source code file in an active AIMFP project (one with .aimfp-project/)? If yes and the file has not been reserved/finalized, you should track it. If this is not a source code file (README, config, docs, etc.) or no AIMFP project is active, no action needed. Respond with {"ok": true} if no action needed, or {"ok": false, "reason": "File should be tracked in project.db via reserve_file/finalize_file"} if tracking is needed.

Before stopping, check: did the user explicitly request to end the session (said 'done', 'wrap up', 'end session', or similar)? If so, has aimfp_end been called for the session audit? If the user requested session end but aimfp_end was not called, respond with {"decision": "block", "reason": "Call aimfp_end for session audit before stopping."}. If this is just a natural end to a response (not an explicit session end request), or if aimfp_end was already called, respond with {"ok": true}. Context: $ARGUMENTS
